docs.title: Phoenix Framework
docs.text: A lightweight 3D game framework built on Silk.NET and OpenGL for C#.

docs.title: What is Phoenix?
docs.text:
Phoenix provides the foundational systems needed to build a 3D game:

The main game loop, cameras, input, audio, collision detection, and an asset pipeline with binary model/texture loading.

It targets .NET 10 and runs on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

docs.title: Core Features
docs.text: System Status Description Game Window Stable Silk.NET window with VSync and resize handling Input Stable Keyboard + mouse via Silk.NET, edge-detect key presses Camera Stable Abstract camera hierarchy with FreeCamera (WASD + mouse) Shaders Stable GLSL vertex/fragment compilation, uniform management, UBO support Textures Stable Binary texture loading with BC1/BC3/BC5 compression support Render Targets Stable Fluent builder API, dynamic sizing, framebuffer blitting Models Stable Assimp-powered binary format, skeleton animation, bone blending UI (ImGui) Stable Overlay rendering, custom fonts, image drawing Gizmos Stable Line-based debug drawing: lines, cubes, spheres, frustums Collision Stable Ray casting, bounding spheres, frustums, AABB/OBB, cylinders Audio Stable OpenAL-based, pluggable decoders (WAV built-in) Asset Pipeline Stable JSON manifest-based loading, cached asset resolution Networking Planned Not yet implemented

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text:
PhoenixGame
 ├── Graphics ──────► RenderTargets, FullScreenQuad, CommonUBO
 ├── InputManager ──► Keyboard, Mouse, Edge-detect
 ├── Camera ────────► View/Projection matrices
 ├── Gizmos ────────► Debug line rendering
 ├── UI ────────────► ImGui overlay + ErrorListWindow
 ├── SoundManager ──► OpenAL audio playback
 └── AssetLoader ───► Cached models, textures, shaders

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide walks you through setting up a Phoenix project from scratch.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• .NET 10 SDK or later
  • A system with an OpenGL-compatible GPU and drivers

docs.title: First, your solution and project structure
docs.text:
mkdir MyPhoenixGame
cd MyPhoenixGame
dotnet new sln -n MyPhoenixGame
dotnet new console -n MyPhoenixGame -o MyPhoenixGame

dotnet sln add MyPhoenixGame/MyPhoenixGame.csproj

docs.title: Now, install the framework and the asset tool.
docs.text:
cd MyPhoenixGame
dotnet add package Phoenix.Framework 
dotnet tool install Phoenix.AssetTool
mkdir Content

docs.title: Using the Phoenix Asset Tool, (pat) initialize an asset manifest
docs.text: dotnet pat Content/asset-manifest.json init

docs.title: In your csproj
docs.text:
Make sure the manifest file and the content files are included on build 

<ItemGroup>
    <None Include="Content\ContentBin\**" CopyToOutputDirectory="PreserveNewest" />
    <None Include="Content\asset-manifest.json" CopyToOutputDirectory="PreserveNewest" />
</ItemGroup>

Require asset build to complete before compilation 

 <Target Name="Phoenix-AssetTool-Build" BeforeTargets="BeforeBuild">
    <Exec Command="dotnet pat Content/asset-manifest.json build" StandardOutputImportance="high" />
  </Target>

docs.title: Understanding the PhoenixGame lifecycle
docs.text: Most systems are internally handled, the game class provides the following methods:

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
• Initialize(): Internal systems ready. Load your assets and configurations here.
  • Update(double deltaTime): Fires each frame. Game logic goes here.
  • Render(double deltaTime): Fires each frame. Rendering goes here.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text:
• InitialLoadScreen(): Fires the first frame, allows you to customize the "Loading game assets" screen before Initialize()
  • OnWindowResize(Vector2 size): Fires when resizing the game window, allows you to handle affected objects.
  • OnClose(): Fires when closing the game, allows you to handle custom object disposal.

docs.title: Set Up the Game Class
docs.text:
Create Game.cs:

using Phoenix.Framework;
using Phoenix.Framework.Cameras;
using Phoenix.Framework.Rendering;
using System.Numerics;

namespace Phoenix.Example;

public class Game : PhoenixGame
{
    Matrix4x4 _cubeWorld = Matrix4x4.Identity;

    protected override void InitialLoadScreen()
    {
        UI.DrawText("Custom startup screen", 
            position: Vector2.Zero, 
            color: Vector4.One, 
            size: 30);
    }
    protected override void Initialize()
    {
        // Asset loading ...
        var cam =  new FreeCamera(
            game: this,
            position: new Vector3(0, 0, -10),
            yaw: MathHelper.PiOver2,
            pitch: 0f,
            fov: MathHelper.PiOver2,
            nearPlane: 1f, 
            farPlane: 1000f,
            aspectRatio: WindowWidth / (float)WindowHeight
        );
        cam.MouseAim = true;

        Camera = cam;

        Gizmos.Enabled = true;

    }

    protected override void Update(double deltaTime)
    {
        // Game logic ...
        var t = (float)Graphics.Time;
        ((FreeCamera)Camera).Update(deltaTime);
        _cubeWorld = Matrix4x4.CreateScale(5f)
            * MathHelper.RotationMxFromYawPitchRoll(t, MathF.Sin(t), MathF.Cos(t));
    }

    protected override void Render(double deltaTime)
    {
        // Render logic ...
        Graphics.SetClearColor(new Vector4(0, 0, 0, 1));
        Graphics.ClearRenderTarget();

        Gizmos.AddCube(_cubeWorld, Vector3.One);
    }

    protected override void RenderUI()
    {
        // UI pass...
        UI.DrawCenteredText("This text is always pixel perfect!", 
            position: new Vector2(WindowWidth/2, 10),
            color: Vector4.One,
            size: 20);
    }

    protected override void OnWindowResize(Vector2 size)
    {
        // Something needs resizing...
    }

    protected override void OnClose()
    {
        // Something needs disposing...
    }
}

docs.title: Entry Point
docs.text:
namespace Phoenix.Example;

public static class Program
{
    public static void Main()
    {
        var game = new Game();
        game.Run();
    }
}

docs.title: Run the Game
docs.text:
dotnet run

A window opens with a black background, the camera is positioned at (0, 0, -10) looking toward the origin. A wireframe cube from Gizmos is rendered spinning around in the center of the screen.

docs.title: AssetTool
docs.text: Phoenix.AssetTool is a companion tool for compiling source assets (FBX models, PNG textures, GLSL shaders) into Phoenix's binary format.

docs.title: CLI Commands
docs.text:
# Initialize an empty manifest, -force to replace
dotnet pat Content/asset-manifest.json init

# Start the AssetTool GUI
dotnet pat Content/asset-manifest.json gui

# Clean the ContentBin folder
dotnet pat Content/asset-manifest.json clean

# add files to the manifest
dotnet pat Content/asset-manifest.json add [path-to-file] [path-to-file2] ...

# add all files in Content/ to the manifest
dotnet pat Content/asset-manifest.json add . 

# remove files from the manifest
dotnet pat Content/asset-manifest.json rem [path-to-file] [path-to-file2] ...

# List all of the files in the manifest
dotnet pat Content/asset-manifest.json list

# Build all of the files in the manifest
dotnet pat Content/asset-manifest.json build

# Automatically track files in the manifest and rebuild changes
dotnet pat Content/asset-manifest.json auto

docs.title: GUI
docs.text: The GUI provides: - File Selector — Opens native file explorer window to select assets - Asset Browser — Browse and preview assets - Build Panel — Build selected assets, see real-time progress - Options (right panel) — Configure model export settings, texture compression - Build Error (right panel) — Hovering on a item that failed build shows a pop-up with the error log, click it to open on the right panel

docs.title: GUI Notes:
docs.text:
• You can select on the top left if you desire light or dark theme.
  • You can resize the font by clicking the buttons in the top left, or by Ctrl+MouseWheel Up/Down.
  • You can add/remove items from build directly from the asset browser.
  • You can resize the left and right panels.
  • In the options for an asset you can run a build process for that item only.

docs.title: Models
docs.text: Input Output Features FBX, OBJ, GLTF, 3DS, DAE, BLEND .bin Static meshes, skeleton animation, UVs, normals, tangents, bone weights

docs.title: Textures
docs.text: Input Output Compression PNG, JPG, BMP, TGA .bin BC1 (DXT1), BC3 (DXT5), BC5 (RGTC2), RGBA8 (uncompressed)

docs.title: Shaders
docs.text:
Input Output .glsl, .vert, .frag .glsl, .vert, .frag (copied as-is)

Shaders are not converted into bin files, they are passed through unchanged. They are, however compiled an verified for errors at build time.

docs.title: Model Load Options
docs.text: Option Default Description IsAnimated false Is an animated model, extract animations Extract Textures true Contains embedded textures, extract them Assimp Flags per option default Postprocessing Flags for assimp

docs.title: Texture Load Options
docs.text: Option Default Description Generate MipMaps true Generate mipmaps for this texture Compression BC3 Target compression format Wrap Horizontal repeat WrapS Wrap Vertical repeat WrapT Min filter Linear Mipmap Linear Interpolation filter min Mag filter Linear Interpolation filter mag

docs.title: Loading Assets
docs.text: Assets are loaded at runtime from a binary content folder resolved via a JSON manifest.

docs.title: AssetLoader
docs.text: Static class for loading models, textures, and shaders.

docs.title: Loading Models
docs.text:
// Load by relative path (extension isnt required)
Model model = AssetLoader.LoadModel("characters/robot");

// Cast to AnimatedModel if needed
AnimatedModel animatedModel = (AnimatedModel)AssetLoader.LoadModel("characters/robot");

docs.title: Loading Textures
docs.text: GLTexture texture = AssetLoader.LoadTexture("textures/brick-albedo");

docs.title: Loading Shaders
docs.text: Handled by Shaders Helpers autogenerated code.

docs.title: Caching
docs.text:
All loaded assets are internally cached in static dictionaries: 

_loadedModels:   Dictionary<string, Model>
_loadedTextures: Dictionary<string, GLTexture>
_loadedShaders:  Dictionary<string, GLShader>
Subsequent calls with the same name return the cached instance: 
var model1 = AssetLoader.LoadModel("characters/robot");
var model2 = AssetLoader.LoadModel("characters/robot");
// model1 == model2  (same instance)

docs.title: AssetManifest
docs.text: A JSON file that maps asset names to file paths.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
    "Assets": [
        {
            "RelativePath": "Models/characters/robot.bin",
            "Type": 0
        },
        {
            "RelativePath": "Textures/brick-albedo",
            "Type": 1
        },
        {
            "RelativePath": "Models/characters/brick-albedo-extracted",
            "Type": 2
        },
        {
          "RelativePath": "Shaders/animated/animated.frag",
          "Type": 3
        },
        {
          "RelativePath": "Shaders/animated/animated.vert",
          "Type": 3
        }
    ],
    "Namespace": "Phoenix.Framework.ShaderHelpers",
    "DarkTheme": true
}

docs.title: AssetType Enum
docs.text: Value Name Description 0 Model Binary model file (.bin) 1 Texture Binary texture file (.bin) 3 ExtTexture Binary texture, extracted from a model (.bin) 3 Shader Shader stage file (.vert / .frag) 4 Unknown Unrecognized asset type

docs.title: Content Folder Structure
docs.text:
Assets get compiled into ContentBin folder, which matches the same structure of Content. 

YourGame/
 ├── Content/                  # Source assets (original files)
 │   ├── characters/
 │   │   └── robot.fbx
 │   └── textures/
 │       └── brick-albedo.png
 ├── ContentBin/               # Compiled binary assets
 │   ├── characters/
 │   │   └── robot.bin
 │   ├── textures/
 │   │   └── brick-albedo.
 └── asset-manifest.json       # Created by you, or generated by AssetTool

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
Shaders are loaded directly as text files, but compiled during Asset building to verify any errors.

If an asset is not found in the manifest, LoadModel(), LoadTexture(), and LoadShader() will throw an exception. If a shader has no matching .vert/.frag pair, it throws with a descriptive error message.

docs.title: Audio
docs.text: Phoenix provides audio playback via OpenAL with a pluggable decoder system.

docs.title: SoundManager (Static API)
docs.text: All audio operations use the static SoundManager class. It is initialized automatically by PhoenixGame during startup.

docs.title: Loading Sounds
docs.text:
// Load a sound clip from disk
var clip = SoundManager.LoadSound("sounds/explosion.wav");

// Load with custom decoder support
var clip = SoundManager.LoadSound("sounds/music.ogg");  // Requires OGG decoder

docs.title: Playing Sounds
docs.text:
// 2D sound (no spatial positioning)
var instance = SoundManager.Play2D(clip);

// 2D with parameters
var instance = SoundManager.Play2D(clip, volume: 0.8f, pitch: 1.0f, loop: false);

// 3D sound (spatialized in world)
var instance = SoundManager.Play3D(clip, new Vector3(5, 1, 0), volume: 0.8f);

docs.title: Playing 3D Sound with Full Control
docs.text:
var instance = SoundManager.Play3D(
    clip,
    position: new Vector3(0, 2, -5),
    volume: 0.7f,
    pitch: 1.2f,
    loop: false
);

docs.title: Controlling Playback
docs.text:
// Start/stop/pause
instance.Play();
instance.Pause();
instance.Stop();

// Adjust properties
instance.SetVolume(0.5f);
instance.SetPitch(1.5f);
instance.SetPosition(new Vector3(1, 2, 3));
instance.SetVelocity(new Vector3(0, 0, 0));

// Check state
bool isPlaying = instance.IsPlaying;

// Clean up when done
instance.Dispose();

docs.title: Listener Configuration
docs.text:
Position the listener (camera) for 3D audio:

// Position
SoundManager.SetListenerPosition(Camera.Position);

// Velocity (for Doppler effect)
SoundManager.SetListenerVelocity(CameraVelocity);

// Orientation (forward + up)
SoundManager.SetListenerOrientation(Camera.Front, Camera.Up);

docs.title: Automatic Cleanup
docs.text: SoundManager.Update() is called automatically by PhoenixGame each frame. It disposes finished instances and cleans up the active list.

docs.title: Custom Decoders
docs.text:
Implement IAudioDecoder to support additional audio formats:

public class OggDecoder : IAudioDecoder
{
    public bool CanDecode(string path)
    {
        return path.EndsWith(".ogg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
    }

    public SoundData Decode(string path)
    {
        // Decode OGG to PCM using a library like StbVorbis
        // Return PCM bytes, format, and sample rate
        return new SoundData(pcmBytes, BufferFormat.Stereo16, 44100);
    }
}

docs.title: Registering a Decoder
docs.text: SoundManager.RegisterDecoder(new OggDecoder());

docs.title: SoundData Structure
docs.text:
public class SoundData
{
    public byte[] PCM { get; }       // Raw PCM audio data
    public BufferFormat Format { get; }  // Audio format
    public int SampleRate { get; }   // Samples per second
}

// BufferFormat values:
// Mono8, Mono16, Stereo8, Stereo16

docs.title: SoundClip
docs.text:
An internal audio buffer created from decoded PCM data. Not directly constructable — use SoundManager.LoadSound() or register a decoder.

public class SoundClip
{
    internal uint BufferId { get; }  // OpenAL buffer handle
}

docs.title: SoundInstance
docs.text:
A playing audio instance tied to a SoundClip.

public class SoundInstance : IDisposable
{
    void Play();
    void Pause();
    void Stop();
    void SetVolume(float volume);
    void SetPitch(float pitch);
    void SetPosition(Vector3 position);      // For 3D sounds
    void SetVelocity(Vector3 velocity);      // For 3D Doppler
    bool IsPlaying { get; }
    void Dispose();
}

docs.title: Built-in Decoder: WAV
docs.text:
The WavDecoder supports: - 8-bit and 16-bit PCM - Mono and Stereo - Any sample rate

Does NOT support: - Compressed formats (MP3, OGG, etc.) - Floating-point PCM - Multi-channel (surround) audio

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
public class MyGame : PhoenixGame
{
    private SoundClip _explosionSound;
    private List<SoundInstance> _activeSounds = new();

    protected override void Initialize()
    {
        _explosionSound = SoundManager.LoadSound("sounds/explosion.wav");
    }

    protected override void Update(double dt)
    {
        // Play explosion at cursor position
        if (InputManager.KeyDownOnce(Key.Space))
        {
            var instance = SoundManager.Play3D(
                _explosionSound,
                Camera.Position + Camera.Front * 5f,
                volume: 0.8f,
                pitch: 1f
            );
            _activeSounds.Add(instance);
        }

        // Clean up finished sounds
        _activeSounds.RemoveAll(s => !s.IsPlaying);
        foreach (var s in _activeSounds)
            s.Dispose();
        _activeSounds.Clear();

        // Update listener position
        SoundManager.SetListenerPosition(Camera.Position);
        SoundManager.SetListenerOrientation(Camera.Front, Camera.Up);
    }
}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AssetLoader — loading sound files from asset manifest
  • ErrorListWindow — audio errors are reported here

docs.title: Frustums & Boxes
docs.text: Covers BoundingFrustum, AxisAlignedBoundingBox, OrientedBoundingBox, and BoundingCylinder.

docs.title: BoundingFrustum
docs.text: Represents the 6-plane view frustum derived from the view-projection matrix.

docs.title: Creating
docs.text:
// From view-projection matrix
var frustum = new BoundingFrustum(Camera.View * Camera.Projection);

docs.title: Updating
docs.text:
// Recalculate planes and corners
frustum.Update(Camera.View * Camera.Projection);

docs.title: Planes
docs.text: Property Type Description Near Plane Near clipping plane Far Plane Far clipping plane Left Plane Left clipping plane Right Plane Right clipping plane Top Plane Top clipping plane Bottom Plane Bottom clipping plane Matrix Matrix4x4 View-projection matrix (triggers recalc) PlaneCount const int 6 CornerCount const int 8

docs.title: Containment Tests
docs.text:
// Point in frustum?
ContainmentType type = frustum.Contains(point);

// Sphere in frustum?
ContainmentType type = frustum.Contains(sphere);
// Returns: Contains (fully inside), Intersects (partially inside), Disjoint (fully outside)

// AABB in frustum?
ContainmentType type = frustum.Contains(aabb);

// Frustum vs frustum?
ContainmentType type = frustum.Contains(otherFrustum);

docs.title: Intersection Tests
docs.text:
bool hits = frustum.Intersects(sphere);
bool hits = frustum.Intersects(aabb);
bool hits = frustum.Intersects(otherFrustum);
PlaneIntersectionType type = frustum.Intersects(plane);
float? dist = frustum.Intersects(ray);

docs.title: Corners
docs.text:
Vector3[] corners = frustum.GetCorners();

// Or into an existing array
Vector3[] corners = new Vector3[8];
frustum.GetCorners(corners);

docs.title: AxisAlignedBoundingBox (AABB)
docs.text: An axis-aligned box defined by min and max corners.

docs.title: Creating
docs.text:
var aabb = new AxisAlignedBoundingBox(
    new Vector3(-1, -1, -1),  // Min corner
    new Vector3(1, 1, 1)       // Max corner
);

// From points
var aabb = AxisAlignedBoundingBox.CreateFromPoints(points);

// From sphere
var aabb = AxisAlignedBoundingBox.CreateFromSphere(sphere);

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description Min Vector3 Minimum corner Max Vector3 Maximum corner Size Vector3 Max - Min Position Vector3 Center (Min + Max) / 2 CornerCount const int 8

docs.title: Corner Access
docs.text: Vector3[] corners = aabb.GetCorners();

docs.title: Containment
docs.text:
ContainmentType type = aabb.Contains(point);
ContainmentType type = aabb.Contains(sphere);
ContainmentType type = aabb.Contains(otherAabb);
ContainmentType type = aabb.Contains(frustum);              // Imprecise
ContainmentType type = aabb.ContainsPrecise(frustum);        // SAT, 23 axes

docs.title: Intersections
docs.text:
bool hits = aabb.Intersects(sphere);
bool hits = aabb.Intersects(otherAabb);
bool hits = aabb.Intersects(frustum);
PlaneIntersectionType type = aabb.Intersects(plane);
float? dist = aabb.Intersects(ray);

docs.title: Transform
docs.text: var transformed = aabb.Transform(worldMatrix);

docs.title: Update
docs.text: aabb.Update(newPosition);  // Move the box

docs.title: OrientedBoundingBox (OBB)
docs.text: An axis-aligned box that can be rotated (oriented by a matrix).

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description Position Vector3 Center Size Vector3 Dimensions Orientation Matrix4x4 Rotation matrix World Matrix4x4 Full world transform (computed)

docs.title: Creating
docs.text:
var obb = new OrientedBoundingBox
{
    Position = new Vector3(0, 1, 0),
    Size = new Vector3(2, 2, 2),
    Orientation = Matrix4x4.Identity
};

// From AABB
var obb = OrientedBoundingBox.FromAABB(aabb);

docs.title: Coordinate Transforms
docs.text:
// World → OBB local space
Vector3 localPoint = obb.ToOBBSpace(worldPoint);

// OBB local space → World
Vector3 worldPoint = obb.ToWorldSpace(localPoint);

docs.title: Intersections
docs.text:
bool hits = obb.Intersects(otherOBB);           // SAT with 15 axes
bool hits = obb.Intersects(aabb);               // AABB-OBB
bool hits = obb.Intersects(sphere);
bool hits = obb.Intersects(ray, out float? dist);  // Ray with distance
bool hits = obb.Intersects(frustum);
PlaneIntersectionType type = obb.Intersects(plane);

docs.title: BoundingCylinder
docs.text: A cylinder with radius, height, and optional rotation.

docs.title: Creating
docs.text:
var cylinder = new BoundingCylinder
{
    Position = new Vector3(0, 1, 0),
    Radius = 1.0f,
    HalfHeight = 2.0f,
    Rotation = Matrix4x4.Identity
};

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description Position Vector3 Cylinder center/base Radius float Cylinder radius HalfHeight float Half height (total = 2 × HalfHeight) Rotation Matrix4x4 Rotation matrix Transform Matrix4x4 Full transform IsXZAligned bool True if aligned to XZ plane (Y-up)

docs.title: Intersections
docs.text:
// Ray intersection
bool hits = cylinder.Intersects(ray);

// Sphere intersection
bool hits = cylinder.Intersects(sphere);

// Point containment
ContainmentType type = cylinder.Contains(point);

// Line segment intersection
Vector3? hit = cylinder.Intersects(pointA, pointB);

// AABB intersection
BoxCylinderIntersection type = cylinder.Intersects(aabb);
// Returns: Edge (touching), Intersecting, None

docs.title: Example: Terrain Collision with Cylinder
docs.text:
// Player collision: test ground cylinder against terrain
var playerCylinder = new BoundingCylinder
{
    Position = playerPosition,
    Radius = 0.5f,
    HalfHeight = 1.8f,
    IsXZAligned = true
};

// Check if cylinder intersects any terrain triangle
foreach (var terrainTriangle in terrainTriangles)
{
    if (playerCylinder.Intersects(terrainTriangle.v[0], terrainTriangle.v[1]) != null)
    {
        // Player is touching terrain at this triangle
        playerPosition.Y = terrainHeight;
        break;
    }
}

docs.text:

docs.title: Collision System — Overview
docs.text: Phoenix provides a comprehensive set of 3D collision volumes and intersection test methods. All volumes are pure math — no physics engine is included.

docs.title: Volumes
docs.text: Volume File Description Ray Ray.cs Origin + direction, for ray casting BoundingSphere BoundingSphere.cs Center + radius AxisAlignedBoundingBox AxisAlignedBoundingBox.cs Min/Max bounds, axis-aligned OrientedBoundingBox OrientedBoundingBox.cs Position + size + orientation matrix BoundingFrustum BoundingFrustum.cs 6-plane view frustum BoundingCylinder BoundingCylinder.cs Position + radius + height + rotation

docs.title: Intersection Test Types
docs.text: Type Return Description bool Intersects(...) bool Do the volumes intersect? ContainmentType Contains(...) Contains/Intersects/Disjoint Full containment check float? Intersects(Ray) float? Distance to hit, or null PlaneIntersectionType Intersects(Plane) Front/Back/Intersecting Which side of a plane

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text:
// Ray casting
Ray ray = new Ray(position, direction);
float? hitDist = ray.Intersects(sphere);
float? hitDist = ray.Intersects(aabb);
float? hitDist = ray.Intersects(plane);
Vector3? triHit = ray.Intersects(v0, v1, v2);  // Triangle (Möller-Trumbore)

// Sphere tests
bool hits = sphere.Intersects(aabb);
bool hits = sphere.Intersects(anotherSphere);
ContainmentType type = sphere.Contains(point);

// AABB tests
bool hits = aabb.Intersects(sphere);
bool hits = aabb.Intersects(frustum);
ContainmentType type = aabb.Contains(sphere);
Vector3[] corners = aabb.GetCorners();

// Frustum tests
bool inFrustum = frustum.Contains(point);
bool inFrustum = frustum.Contains(sphere);
bool inFrustum = frustum.Contains(aabb);
Vector3[] corners = frustum.GetCorners();

// OBB tests
bool hits = obb.Intersects(aabb);
bool hits = obb.Intersects(obb);
bool hits = obb.Intersects(ray, out float? dist);

// Cylinder tests
bool hits = cylinder.Intersects(ray);
bool hits = cylinder.Intersects(sphere);
ContainmentType type = cylinder.Contains(point);

docs.title: Drawing Volumes with Gizmos
docs.text:
All collision volumes can be visualized using Gizmos.AddVolume<T>():

Gizmos.AddVolume(sphere, new Vector3(1, 0, 0));
Gizmos.AddVolume(aabb, new Vector3(0, 1, 0));
Gizmos.AddVolume(obb, new Vector3(0, 0, 1));
Gizmos.AddVolume(frustum, new Vector3(1, 1, 0));
Gizmos.AddVolume(cylinder, new Vector3(1, 0.5f, 0.5f));

docs.title: From Model Data
docs.text:
// Create bounding sphere from model vertices
var sphere = BoundingSphere.CreateFromPoints(modelVertices);

// Create AABB from points
var aabb = AxisAlignedBoundingBox.CreateFromPoints(modelVertices);

// Create sphere from AABB
var sphereFromBox = BoundingSphere.CreateFromBoundingBox(aabb);

// Create sphere from frustum
var sphereFromFrustum = BoundingSphere.CreateFromFrustum(frustum);

// Create AABB from sphere
var aabbFromSphere = AxisAlignedBoundingBox.CreateFromSphere(sphere);

// Merge two spheres
var merged = BoundingSphere.CreateMerged(sphereA, sphereB);

// OBB from AABB
var obb = OrientedBoundingBox.FromAABB(aabb);

docs.title: From Transform
docs.text:
// Transform a sphere by a matrix
var transformed = sphere.Transform(worldMatrix);

// Transform a box by a matrix
var transformedBox = aabb.Transform(worldMatrix);

// Transform a frustum
frustum.Update(Camera.View * Camera.Projection);

docs.title: Coordinate Space Conversions (OBB)
docs.text:
// Convert world point to OBB local space
Vector3 localPoint = obb.ToOBBSpace(worldPoint);

// Convert local point to world space
Vector3 worldPoint = obb.ToWorldSpace(localPoint);

docs.title: Plane Helpers
docs.text:
The PlaneHelper utility provides plane transformations and distance calculations:

// Classify a point relative to a plane
PlaneIntersectionType type = PlaneHelper.ClassifyPoint(plane, point);

// Get perpendicular distance from point to plane
float distance = PlaneHelper.PerpendicularDistance(plane, point);

// Transform a plane by a matrix
Plane transformed = PlaneHelper.Transform(plane, matrix);

// Transform a plane by a quaternion
Plane transformed = PlaneHelper.Transform(plane, rotation);

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Ray Casting — detailed ray intersection guide
  • Bounding Spheres — sphere operations
  • Frustums & Boxes — frustum, AABB, OBB, cylinder
  • Gizmos — visualize collision volumes

docs.title: Ray Casting
docs.text: The Ray class represents a ray with an origin and direction, used for line-of-sight tests, projectile trajectories, and mouse picking.

docs.title: Creating a Ray
docs.text:
Ray ray = new Ray(
    new Vector3(0, 5, -10),   // Origin (camera position)
    new Vector3(0, -0.1f, 1)  // Direction (normalized)
);

docs.title: Ray vs Triangle (Möller–Trumbore)
docs.text:
Returns the hit point if the ray intersects the triangle, or null:

Vector3? hit = ray.Intersects(v0, v1, v2);
if (hit.HasValue)
{
    Vector3 intersectionPoint = hit.Value;
    // The ray hit the triangle at intersectionPoint
}

docs.title: Ray vs BoundingSphere
docs.text:
Returns distance to intersection (closest hit), or null:

BoundingSphere sphere = new BoundingSphere(center, radius);
float? distance = ray.Intersects(sphere);
if (distance.HasValue)
{
    // Hit at distance units from ray origin
    Vector3 hitPoint = ray.Position + ray.Direction * distance.Value;
}

docs.title: Ray vs AxisAlignedBoundingBox
docs.text:
AxisAlignedBoundingBox box = new AxisAlignedBoundingBox(min, max);
float? distance = ray.Intersects(box);
if (distance.HasValue)
{
    // Box hit at distance
}

docs.title: Ray vs Plane
docs.text:
// Create a plane from a point and normal
Plane plane = new Plane(normal, D);  // or from 3 points
float? distance = ray.Intersects(plane);
if (distance.HasValue)
{
    Vector3 hitPoint = ray.Position + ray.Direction * distance.Value;
}

docs.title: Ray vs Line Segment
docs.text:
float? distance = ray.Intersects(lineOrigin, lineDestination);
if (distance.HasValue)
{
    // Ray hit the line segment
}

docs.title: Mouse Picking Example
docs.text:
Convert a screen coordinate to a world-space ray:

protected override void Update(double dt)
{
    if (InputManager.KeyDownOnce(Key.MouseLeft))
    {
        Vector2 mousePos = InputManager.MousePosition;  // Screen space

        // Convert to normalized device coordinates (-1 to +1)
        float ndcX = (mousePos.X / WindowWidth) * 2f - 1f;
        float ndcY = 1f - (mousePos.Y / WindowHeight) * 2f;

        // Unproject using inverse view-projection
        Matrix4x4 invVP = Matrix4x4.Invert(Camera.View * Camera.Projection);
        Vector3 near = new Vector3(ndcX, ndcY, 0f);
        Vector3 far = new Vector3(ndcX, ndcY, 1f);

        Vector3 nearWorld = Vector3.TransformCoordinate(near, invVP);
        Vector3 farWorld = Vector3.TransformCoordinate(far, invVP);

        Ray ray = new Ray(nearWorld, (farWorld - nearWorld).Normal());

        // Test against scene objects
        float? hitDist = ray.Intersects(enemyCollisionSphere);
        if (hitDist.HasValue)
        {
            // Enemy was clicked!
            enemy.TakeDamage(25);
        }
    }
}

docs.title: Ray Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description Position Vector3 Ray origin Direction Vector3 Ray direction (should be normalized)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The ray direction does not need to be normalized for intersection tests (the algorithm normalizes internally).
  • Distance is measured along the ray direction from the origin.
  • If the ray origin is inside a sphere or box, distance is 0.
  • Triangle intersection uses the Möller–Trumbore algorithm (barycentric coordinates).

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Bounding Spheres — BoundingSphere.Intersects(Ray)
  • Frustums & Boxes — other volume ray tests
  • Gizmos — visualize rays with AddLine

docs.title: Bounding Spheres
docs.text: A BoundingSphere is defined by a center point and a radius. It provides fast containment and intersection tests.

docs.title: Creating a Sphere
docs.text:
// From center and radius
var sphere = new BoundingSphere(new Vector3(0, 1, 0), 2.5f);

docs.title: From Other Volumes
docs.text:
// From a set of points
var sphere = BoundingSphere.CreateFromPoints(vertexPositions);

// From an AABB
var sphere = BoundingSphere.CreateFromBoundingBox(aabb);

// From a frustum
var sphere = BoundingSphere.CreateFromFrustum(frustum);

// Merge two spheres (tightest bound containing both)
var merged = BoundingSphere.CreateMerged(sphereA, sphereB);

docs.title: Transforming
docs.text:
// Transform by matrix (e.g., world transform)
var transformed = sphere.Transform(worldMatrix);

docs.title: Updating
docs.text:
// Move the sphere center
sphere.Update(newPosition);

docs.title: Sphere vs Sphere
docs.text:
bool hits = sphere.Intersects(otherSphere);
ContainmentType type = sphere.Contains(otherSphere);  // Contains / Intersects / Disjoint

docs.title: Sphere vs AABB
docs.text:
bool hits = sphere.Intersects(aabb);
ContainmentType type = sphere.Contains(aabb);

docs.title: Sphere vs Frustum
docs.text: ContainmentType type = sphere.Contains(frustum);

docs.title: Sphere vs Plane
docs.text:
PlaneIntersectionType type = sphere.Intersects(plane);
// Returns: Front (center is in front), Back (center is behind), Intersecting (crosses)

docs.title: Sphere vs Ray
docs.text:
Ray ray = new Ray(origin, direction);
float? distance = sphere.Intersects(ray);
if (distance.HasValue)
{
    // Ray hits the sphere at distance units
}

docs.title: Sphere vs Point
docs.text:
ContainmentType type = sphere.Contains(point);
// Returns: Contains (point inside), Disjoint (point outside)

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description Center Vector3 Sphere center Radius float Sphere radius

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Enemy hitboxes — fast overlap checks
  • View culling — frustum.Contains(sphere) to skip off-screen objects
  • Spatial partitioning — sphere bounds for quadtree/octree nodes
  • Proximity checks — sphere.Intersects(otherSphere) for trigger zones

docs.title: Example: View Culling
docs.text:
foreach (var enemy in enemies)
{
    // Skip enemies outside the camera frustum
    if (frustum.Contains(enemy.CollisionSphere) == ContainmentType.Disjoint)
        continue;

    // Enemy is visible — render it
    RenderEnemy(enemy);
}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Ray Casting — BoundingSphere.Intersects(Ray)
  • Frustums & Boxes — frustum contains sphere test
  • Gizmos — AddVolume(sphere, color)

docs.title: Camera
docs.text: Phoenix provides a hierarchy of camera classes for viewport control. Cameras feed View and Projection matrices into the CommonUBO every frame.

docs.title: Camera Hierarchy
docs.text:
Camera (abstract)
 └── BaseCamera (abstract)
      └── MouseCamera (abstract)
           └── FreeCamera (concrete)
Class Purpose Camera Base class defining View and Projection BaseCamera Shared state: position, yaw, pitch, FOV, aspect ratio MouseCamera Adds mouse delta input for yaw/pitch control FreeCamera WASD-style movement + mouse look

docs.title: FreeCamera
docs.text: The main camera class. Supports keyboard movement and mouse look.

docs.title: Creating a FreeCamera
docs.text:
Camera = new FreeCamera(
    this,                           // PhoenixGame reference
    new Vector3(0, 5, -10),         // Position
    0,                              // Yaw (radians)
    -MathF.PI / 4,                  // Pitch (radians)
    MathF.PI / 4,                   // Max pitch (prevents flipping)
    0.1f,                           // Near plane
    1000f,                          // Far plane
    WindowWidth / (float)WindowHeight  // Aspect ratio
);

docs.title: Movement Keys
docs.text:
// WASD + QE (up/down) + Shift (sprint)
camera.SetMoveKeys(
    Key.W,    // Forward
    Key.S,    // Backward
    Key.A,    // Left
    Key.D,    // Right
    Key.E,    // Up
    Key.Q,    // Down
    Key.LeftShift,  // Speed multiplier key
    moveSpeedMultiplier: 2f   // Sprint multiplier
);

camera.MoveSpeed = 15f;  // Base movement speed

docs.title: Mouse Look
docs.text:
Mouse look is enabled by default (MouseAim = true). Mouse delta is automatically applied to yaw and pitch each frame.

camera.MouseSensitivity = 0.002f;  // Override default (0.001f)

docs.title: Manual Yaw/Pitch Keys
docs.text:
For keyboards without mouse, you can set arrow keys for yaw/pitch:

camera.SetPitchYawKeys(
    Key.Up,    // Increase pitch
    Key.Down,  // Decrease pitch
    Key.Left,  // Decrease yaw
    Key.Right, // Increase yaw
    new Vector2(2f, 2f)  // Speed multipliers [pitch, yaw]
);

docs.title: Updating the Camera
docs.text:
Call Update() every frame in your Update() method:

protected override void Update(double dt)
{
    Camera.Update((float)dt);
}

This processes: - Mouse delta → yaw/pitch rotation - Keyboard input → position translation - Recalculates View matrix (LookAt) - Recalculates Projection matrix (perspective)

docs.title: Camera Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description Position Vector3 Camera position Front Vector3 Forward direction (normalized) Up Vector3 Up vector (normalized) Right Vector3 Right vector (normalized) Yaw float Horizontal rotation (radians) Pitch float Vertical rotation (radians) FOV float Field of view (radians) AspectRatio float Width / Height NearPlane float Near clipping plane FarPlane float Far clipping plane View Matrix4x4 View matrix (set each frame) Projection Matrix4x4 Projection matrix (set each frame) MoveSpeed float Movement speed (default 10) MouseAim bool Whether to process mouse delta (default true) MouseSensitivity float Mouse sensitivity (default 0.001)

docs.title: Camera Usage
docs.text:
The camera's View and Projection matrices are automatically fed into the CommonUBO every frame. Shaders that need camera data bind this UBO:

// In your shader:
layout(std140) uniform CommonData
{
    mat4 sView;
    mat4 sProjection;
    float sTime;
    float sDeltaTime;
};

// gl_Position = sProjection * sView * World * vPosition;

docs.title: Custom Camera
docs.text:
For custom camera behavior, inherit from BaseCamera:

public class OrbitCamera : BaseCamera
{
    public float Distance;

    public OrbitCamera(PhoenixGame game, Vector3 target, float distance,
        float yaw, float pitch, float fov, float nearPlane, float farPlane,
        float aspectRatio)
        : base(game, target, distance, yaw, pitch, fov, nearPlane, farPlane, aspectRatio)
    {
        Distance = distance;
    }

    public override void Update(float deltaTime)
    {
        // Custom orbit logic
        CalculateVectors();
        CalculateView();
        CalculateProjection();
    }
}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Gizmos — gizmos use Camera.View and Camera.Projection
  • BoundingFrustum — frustum is built from Camera.View * Camera.Projection

docs.title: PhoenixGame
docs.text: PhoenixGame is the abstract base class for all Phoenix applications. It manages the window, input, rendering pipeline, and coordinates all subsystems.

docs.title: Understanding the internal pipeline
docs.text: The windowing manager from Silk.NET fires internally InternalLoad(), InternalUpdate(), and InternalRender(). These will handle setting up and updating all internal systems every frame in the background as shown below.

docs.title: Internal systems
docs.text:
Run()
 └── Window.Create()
      └── Window.Load       → InternalLoad()
           ├── InputManager created
           ├── UI created (ImGui initialized)
           ├── RTManager created
           ├── Scene render target created
           ├── Graphics created
           └── CommonUBO created
      └── Window.Update     → InternalUpdate()
           ├── Frame 0: DelayedLoad()
           │    ├── FullScreenQuad created
           │    ├── Gizmos created
           │    ├── SoundManager initialized
           │    ├── AssetLoader initialized
           │    └── Initialize() called
           │
           ├── Graphics.Time += dt
           ├── InputManager.Update()
           ├── Render halt check (F11)
           ├── Update(dt)          ← user code
           ├── CommonUBO updated
           └── Gizmos.Update() if enabled
      └── Window.Render     → InternalRender()
           ├── UI.Update(dt)
           ├── If not halted:
           │    ├── SetRenderToTarget(_sceneRT)
           │    ├── Render(dt)           ← user code
           │    └── Gizmos.Render() if enabled
           └── TrueRenderToScreen()
               ├── ClearRenderTarget()
               ├── UI.Render()           ← ImGui overlay
               └── RenderUI(dt)          ← user overlay code

docs.title: The internal scene target and RenderViewport
docs.text: The pipeline is designed to have the game render to an internal scene render target. This allows the UI layer of the game to render using the game window resolution, keeping the UI clean and pixel perfect, independent of the rendering framebuffer size which can be set to a different (lower) resolution at Graphics.RenderViewport.

docs.title: Rendering halt
docs.text: This internal control allows the game to be halted at any time using the configured key (F11 by default) which stops excecuting the update and render methods but keeps the UI alive and responsive. Useful for debugging purposes.

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text: • The CommonUBO is updated every frame with [View, Projection, Time, DeltaTime] and bound at binding point 0.

docs.title: Game Access
docs.text: Property Type Notes GL GL Silk.NET OpenGL context (available after Load) Window IWindow Silk.NET window handle WindowSize Vector2 Current window dimensions WindowWidth int Width shortcut WindowHeight int Height shortcut

docs.title: System Access
docs.text: Property Type Notes InputManager InputManager Read-only. Do not assign. FullScreenQuad FullScreenQuad Read-only. Single quad for post-processing. Gizmos Gizmos Read-only. Debug drawing. UI UI Read-only. ImGui and text overlay. Camera Camera Settable. Assign your camera instance in Initialize(). Graphics Graphics Read-only after load. CommonUboHandle uint UBO handle bound at binding point 0.

docs.title: Graphics & Rendering
docs.text: The Graphics class manages OpenGL state, render targets, and the rendering pipeline.

docs.title: GL State Management
docs.text: Graphics tracks previous state to avoid redundant GL calls. All methods are idempotent.

docs.title: Depth Testing
docs.text:
Graphics.SetDepthTest(true);
Graphics.SetDepthTest(true, GLEnum.Less);  // with custom compare function

docs.title: Alpha Blending
docs.text:
Graphics.SetAlphaBlend(true);
Graphics.SetAlphaBlend(true,
    BlendingFactor.SrcAlpha,
    BlendingFactor.OneMinusSrcAlpha);

docs.title: Face Culling
docs.text:
Graphics.SetFaceCulling(true);
Graphics.SetFaceCulling(true, GLEnum.Back, frontIsCcw: true);

docs.title: Clearing and Viewport
docs.text:
Graphics.SetClearColor(new Vector4(0.1f, 0.15f, 0.2f, 1f));
Graphics.ClearRenderTarget();
Graphics.ClearRenderTarget(true, true, false);  // color + depth

docs.title: Accessors
docs.text: Property Type Description Time double Total elapsed time (set internally) FPS double Instantaneous FPS FPS_SAMPLE double Averaged FPS over FPS_SAMPLE_RATE (default 0.3s) FT_SAMPLE double Frame time sample RenderHaltKey Key Key that toggles render halt (default Key.F11)

docs.title: Render Targets
docs.text: Render targets redirect rendering to an off-screen framebuffer. The framework provides fluent builders.

docs.title: Creating a Render Target
docs.text:
// Simple render target with default 1-texture + depth
var rt = Graphics.BuildRenderTarget()
    .AddTexture()
    .AddDepthBuffer()
    .Build();

// Abstraction that internally does the above.
var rt = Graphics.NewRenderTarget();

// Custom render target with named texture and specific settings
var rt = Graphics.BuildRenderTarget()
    .SetName("post-process-rt")
    .AddTexture(RTManager.BuildRTT()
        .SetFormat(GLEnum.Rgba8)
        .SetWrapS(GLEnum.ClampToEdge)
        .SetMinFilter(GLEnum.Linear)
        .SetStatic(new Vector2(1024, 1024)))
    .SetDepthBuffer(new DepthBuffer())
    .Build();

// Dynamic size (follows window scaling)
var rt = Graphics.BuildRenderTarget()
    .SetName("half-res-rt")
    .AddTexture(RTManager.BuildRTT()
        .SetResolutionMultiplier(new Vector2(0.5f, 0.5f)))
    .AddDepthBuffer()
    .Build();

docs.title: Depth Buffer
docs.text:
new DepthBuffer()                              // Window-sized, Depth24Stencil8
new DepthBuffer(GLEnum.Depth24Stencil8)
new DepthBuffer(new Vector2(1024, 1024))  // Fixed size

docs.title: Using Render Targets
docs.text:
// Redirect rendering to a target
Graphics.SetRenderToTarget(rt);
// ... draw to rt ...

// Return to screen
Graphics.SetRenderToScreen();
- Remember the scene is always rendered to an internal _sceneRT each frame. SetRenderToScreen() selects this rt as the target.

docs.title: Finding Render Targets
docs.text:
if (Graphics.TryFindByName("post-process-rt", out var target))
{
    // Use target
}

var target = Graphics.FindByName("post-process-rt");  // throws if not found

docs.title: FullScreenQuad
docs.text:
A single quad from -1 to 1 with UV coordinates 0→1. Used for post-processing:

Graphics.FullScreenQuad.Draw();  // Draws the quad

docs.title: CommonUBO
docs.text:
A single Uniform Buffer Object (at binding point 0) internally updated every frame with camera and timing data:

// GLSL side
layout(std140) uniform CommonData {
    mat4 sView;           // layout(location = 0)
    mat4 sProjection;     // layout(location = 1)
    float sTime;          // layout(location = 2)
    float sDeltaTime;     // layout(location = 3)
};

// C# side — the UBO is created and updated automatically by PhoenixGame.
// Bind it in your shader (auto gen shader helpers have this already.)
shader.AttachUBO(Game.CommonUboHandle, "CommonData", binding: 0);

docs.title: RenderViewport
docs.text:
Controls the scale of render targets relative to the window:

Graphics.RenderViewport.Scale = new Vector2(0.5f, 0.5f);  // Half resolution

docs.title: Input
docs.text: The InputManager handles keyboard and mouse input via Silk.NET.

docs.title: Access
docs.text:
InputManager is a read-only property on PhoenixGame:

var input = InputManager;  // or: this.InputManager

docs.title: Key State Queries
docs.text:
// Is the key currently held down this frame?
if (InputManager.KeyDown(Key.W))
{
    // Move forward
}

// Fires only the first frame a key is down, 
// Resets when releasing it.
if (InputManager.KeyDownOnce(Key.E))
{
    // Toggle something...
    InputManager.ToggleMouseMode();
}

docs.title: Mouse Delta
docs.text:
Accumulated mouse movement this frame, multiplied by sensitivity:

Vector2 delta = InputManager.MouseDelta;
// delta.X = horizontal movement
// delta.Y = vertical movement

docs.title: Mouse Wheel
docs.text:
protected override void Update(double dt)
{
    float wheel = InputManager.MouseWheelValue;
    if (wheel != 0)
    {
        // Zoom in/out
        Camera.FOV -= wheel * 0.05f;
    }
}

docs.title: Mouse Mode
docs.text:
Lock the cursor to the window (raw input) or allow free movement:

// Lock cursor (raw mouse input)
InputManager.SetMouseMode(CursorMode.Raw);

// Unlock cursor (normal mode)
InputManager.SetMouseMode(CursorMode.Normal);

// Toggle between the two
InputManager.ToggleMouseMode();

docs.title: Mouse Sensitivity
docs.text: InputManager.MouseSensitivity = 0.002f;  // Default: 0.001f

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
protected override void Update(double dt)
{
    // Toggle mouse lock
    if (InputManager.KeyDownOnce(Key.Escape))
        InputManager.ToggleMouseMode();

    // Camera rotation from mouse delta
    if (InputManager.MouseAim)
    {
        Camera.Yaw += InputManager.MouseDelta.X * InputManager.MouseSensitivity;
        Camera.Pitch += InputManager.MouseDelta.Y * InputManager.MouseSensitivity;
    }

    // Edge-detect interaction
    if (InputManager.KeyDownOnce(Key.E))
    {
        InteractWithClosestObject();
    }

    // Continuous action
    if (InputManager.KeyDown(Key.LeftShift))
    {
        MoveForward(15f * (float)dt);  // Sprint
    }
    else
    {
        MoveForward(5f * (float)dt);   // Walk
    }

    // Mouse wheel zoom
    if (InputManager.MouseWheelValue != 0)
    {
        Camera.FOV = MathHelper.Clamp(Camera.FOV + InputManager.MouseWheelValue, 0.1f, MathF.PI / 2f);
    }
}

docs.title: Internal Behavior
docs.text:
• KeyDown() returns true for the frame a key is first pressed AND every frame it is held.
  • KeyDownOnce() returns true only on the frame the key is first pressed (edge detection).
  • MouseDelta is accumulated from the OnMouseMove event and reset each Update().
  • MouseWheelValue is reset each frame after being read.
  • keysDown list tracks which keys have been pressed / released each frame.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Camera — FreeCamera uses InputManager.MouseDelta for rotation
  • Gizmos — F11 toggles render halt via InputManager

MathHelper.TwoPi         // MathF.PI * 2.0f
MathHelper.PiOver2       // MathF.PI / 2.0f
MathHelper.PiOver4       // MathF.PI / 4.0f

float degs = MathF.PI.ToDeg();  // → 180f

float lerp = 0.5f.Lerp(0f, 1f);      // Linear interpolation: 0.5
float wrapped = 400f.WrapAngle(360f); // Wrap angle: 40

// Note: WrapAngle takes modulus value, not a range

docs.title: String Formatting

string s = vector3.ToStr();        // "1.0000, 2.0000, 3.0000"
string s = vector3.ToStrF2();      // "1.00, 2.00, 3.00"
string s = vector3.ToStrInt();     // "1, 2, 3"

string s = matrix.ToStrF2();      // 2 decimal places

docs.title: Transformation
docs.text:
// In-place inverse
matrix.Invert();
matrix.InverseTranspose();  // Invert then transpose (for normal matrices)
matrix.Transpose();

// Yaw/pitch/roll rotation matrix

// Create from Euler angles
Quaternion q = Quaternion.RotationFromYawPitchRoll(yaw, pitch, roll);

// Decompose to Euler angles
q.ExtractYawPitchRoll(out float yaw, out float pitch, out float roll);

docs.title: Bone Name Trimming
docs.text:
string clean = "mixamorig:Hips".TrimBoneName();
// Result: "Hips"
// Removes common Mixamo prefix patterns: "mixamorig:", "mixamorig:", "mixamo:", etc.

docs.text:
var floats = vertexPositions.ToFloatArrayList();
// Flattens [v1, v2, v3] → [v1.X, v1.Y, v1.Z, v2.X, v2.Y, v2.Z, ...]

Vector2D Extensions 
float num = vector2D.ToNum();  // Returns X as the numeric value

See Also

  • Shaders — Matrix4x4.ToFloatArray() for passing matrices to uniforms
Gizmos
docs.text: Gizmos provide debug visualization for geometry, collisions, and spatial data. They render as colored wireframes on top of the scene.

docs.title: Access
docs.text: var gizmos = Gizmos;  // Read-only from PhoenixGame

docs.title: Enabling/Disabling
docs.text:
Gizmos.Enabled = true;   // Default: true
Gizmos.Enabled = false;  // Skips Update() and Render() when false

When disabled, gizmo drawing is completely skipped each frame.

docs.title: Lines
docs.text:
// Draw a line from origin to destination
Gizmos.AddLine(
    Vector3.Zero,           // origin
    new Vector3(0, 5, 0),   // destination
    new Vector3(1, 1, 1)    // color (RGB, 0-1)
);

// Red line (hit indicator)
Gizmos.AddLine(Vector3.Zero, new Vector3(10, 0, 0), new Vector3(1, 0, 0), hit: true);

docs.title: Cubes
docs.text:
// Axis-aligned cube
Gizmos.AddCube(
    Vector3.Zero,           // center position
    new Vector3(2, 2, 2),   // size (width, height, depth)
    new Vector3(0, 1, 0)    // color
);

// Oriented cube (from a transform matrix)
Gizmos.AddCube(worldMatrix, new Vector3(1, 1, 0));

docs.title: Spheres
docs.text:
// Bounding sphere visualization
Gizmos.AddSphere(
    new Vector3(0, 1, 0),   // center
    2.5f,                   // radius
    new Vector3(0, 0, 1)    // color
);

// From a matrix (e.g., object transform)
Gizmos.AddSphere(enemy.Transform, new Vector3(1, 1, 0));

docs.title: Cylinders
docs.text:
// Cylinder with explicit parameters
Gizmos.AddCylinder(
    Vector3.Zero,           // base position
    1.0f,                   // radius
    3.0f,                   // height
    Quaternion.Identity,    // rotation
    new Vector3(1, 0.5f, 0) // color
);

// From matrix (oriented cylinder)
Gizmos.AddCylinder(tree.Transform, new Vector3(0, 1, 1));

docs.title: Planes
docs.text:
// Axis-aligned plane (facing up)
Gizmos.AddPlane(
    Vector3.Zero,           // position
    Vector3.UnitY,          // normal
    new Vector2(5, 5),      // size (x, z extent)
    new Vector3(1, 1, 0)    // color
);

// From matrix (oriented plane)
Gizmos.AddPlane(ground.Transform, new Vector3(1, 1, 0));

docs.title: Collision Volumes
docs.text:
Any collision volume can be drawn using the generic method:

// Sphere
Gizmos.AddVolume(sphere, new Vector3(0, 1, 0));

// AABB
Gizmos.AddVolume(aabb, new Vector3(1, 0, 0));

// OBB
Gizmos.AddVolume(obb, new Vector3(0, 0, 1));

// Cylinder
Gizmos.AddVolume(cylinder, new Vector3(1, 1, 0));

// Frustum (camera view volume)
Gizmos.AddVolume(frustum, new Vector3(1, 0.5f, 0.5f));

docs.title: Axis Lines
docs.text:
Draw coordinate axes at the origin:

Gizmos.AddAxisLines(5);  // Length 5 units

docs.title: Color System
docs.text:
Colors use RGB values (0–1 range):

new Vector3(1, 0, 0)  // Red
new Vector3(0, 1, 0)  // Green
new Vector3(0, 0, 1)  // Blue
new Vector3(1, 1, 0)  // Yellow
new Vector3(1, 0, 1)  // Magenta
new Vector3(0, 1, 1)  // Cyan
new Vector3(1, 1, 1)  // White
new Vector3(0.5f, 0.5f, 0.5f)  // Gray

The hit parameter inverts colors (used for collision visualization):

// Normal (green) → Hit (red)
Gizmos.AddVolume(sphere, new Vector3(0, 1, 0), hit: true);

docs.title: Gizmo Geometries
docs.text: Type Internal Class Vertices Lines Line GGLineSegment 2 1 Cube GGCube 8 12 Sphere GGSphere 64×3 circles 192 edges Cylinder GGCylinder 64×3 circles + 4 vertical 196 edges Plane GGPlane 4 5 (rectangle + diagonal)

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
protected override void Render(double dt)
{
    // Draw the scene
    RenderScene();

    // Debug: draw camera frustum
    var frustum = new BoundingFrustum(Camera.View * Camera.Projection);
    Gizmos.AddVolume(frustum, new Vector3(1, 0.5f, 0.5f));

    // Debug: draw all enemy collision spheres
    foreach (var enemy in enemies)
    {
        Gizmos.AddVolume(enemy.CollisionSphere, new Vector3(1, 0, 0));
    }

    // Debug: draw ray to closest enemy
    var closest = GetClosestEnemy();
    if (closest != null)
    {
        Gizmos.AddLine(Camera.Position, closest.Position, new Vector3(0, 1, 0));
    }

    // Debug: draw grid on ground
    for (int x = -10; x <= 10; x++)
    {
        Gizmos.AddLine(new Vector3(x, 0, -10), new Vector3(x, 0, 10), new Vector3(0.3f, 0.3f, 0.3f));
        Gizmos.AddLine(new Vector3(-10, 0, x), new Vector3(10, 0, x), new Vector3(0.3f, 0.3f, 0.3f));
    }
}

docs.title: Models & Animation
docs.text: Phoenix loads 3D models from binary format (compiled from Assimp by AssetTool). Supports static meshes and skeletal animation.

docs.title: Loading Models
docs.text:
// Load a static model
var staticModel = (Model)AssetLoader.LoadModel("props/box");

// Load an animated model
var animatedModel = (AnimatedModel)AssetLoader.LoadModel("characters/walk");

docs.title: Model Hierarchy
docs.text:
Model
 ├── Parts: List<ModelPart>
 │     └── Name: string
 │     └── Meshes: List<ModelMesh>
 │           └── Name: string
 │           └── Transform: Matrix4x4
 │           └── Draw()
 └── TextureNames: List<string>

AnimatedModel : Model
 ├── Animations: List<Animation>
 ├── AnimatorNodes: AnimatorNode[]
 ├── InverseGlobalTransform: Matrix4x4[]
 ├── BoneCount: int
 ├── FinalBoneMatrices: Matrix4x4[]
 ├── SetAnimation(int index)
 └── Update(float deltaTime)

docs.title: Rendering a Static Model
docs.text:
var model = (Model)AssetLoader.LoadModel("props/tree");

foreach (var part in model.Parts)
{
    foreach (var mesh in part.Meshes)
    {
        // Set your shader uniforms (model matrix, textures, etc.)
        ShaderBasicModel.Use();
        ShaderBasicModel.World.Set(mesh.Transform);
        ShaderBasicModel.Albedo.Set(albedoTexture);

        // Draw the mesh
        mesh.Draw();
    }
}

docs.title: Rendering an Animated Model
docs.text:
var model = (AnimatedModel)AssetLoader.LoadModel("characters/robot");
model.SetAnimation(0);  // Select animation by index

protected override void Update(double dt)
{
    model.Update((float)dt);  // Advance animation, compute bone transforms
}

protected override void Render(double dt)
{
    foreach (var part in model.Parts)
    {
        foreach (var mesh in part.Meshes)
        {
            // Pass bone matrices to shader
            ShaderAnimated.Use();
            ShaderAnimated.BoneMatrices.Set(model.FinalBoneMatrices));
            mesh.Draw();
        }
    }
}

docs.title: AnimatedModel Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description Animations List<Animation> All animations in the model AnimatorNodes AnimatorNode[] Bone hierarchy nodes InverseGlobalTransform Matrix4x4[] Bone offset matrices BoneCount int Number of bones FinalBoneMatrices Matrix4x4[] Final world-space bone matrices (updated by Update)

docs.title: Animations
docs.text: Each Animation represents one animation clip (e.g., "Idle", "Walk", "Attack").

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description Name string Animation name Duration float Total duration in seconds TicksPerSecond float Keyframe ticks per second CurrentFrame Transform[] Interpolated transforms per bone Transforms Matrix4x4[] Final matrix per bone

docs.title: Playing Animations
docs.text:
model.SetAnimation(0);  // First animation (0-indexed)
model.SetAnimation(1);  // Second animation

// Access animation properties
var anim = model.Animations[0];
Console.WriteLine($"Duration: {anim.Duration}s, Name: {anim.Name}");

docs.title: Keyframe Structure
docs.text:
Each animation contains per-bone keyframe arrays:

Animation
 └── BoneKeyframes: Keyframe[][]
       └── Keyframe
            ├── TimeStamp: float
            └── Transform: Transform
                 ├── Scale: Vector3
                 ├── Rotation: Quaternion
                 └── Translation: Vector3

docs.title: ModelMesh
docs.text: Each mesh within a model part. Contains vertex data (VAO) and index data.

docs.title: Vertex Layout
docs.text: Attribute Type Location Position Vector3 0 Normal Vector3 1 TexCoord Vector2 2 Tangent Vector3 3 Bitangent Vector3 4 BoneIds Vector4<int> 5 Weights Vector4<float> 6

docs.title: Drawing
docs.text: mesh.Draw();  // GL.BindVertexArray + GL.DrawElements

docs.title: Bone Animation Details
docs.text:
Bone transforms are computed in AnimatedModel.Update(deltaTime):

  1. Interpolate keyframes for each bone based on elapsed time
  2. Apply local bone transforms (scale, rotation, translation)
  3. Propagate transforms down the bone hierarchy (parent × child)
  4. Combine with InverseGlobalTransform offsets for final world-space matrices
  5. Store results in FinalBoneMatrices[] for the shader

The shader applies skinning using the 4 bone IDs and weights per vertex:

// In vertex shader:
for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
    skinPos += boneMatrices[int(boneIds[i])][i] * boneWeights[i];
}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AssetLoader — loading models by name
  • AssetTool — compiling models to binary format
  • Shaders — passing bone matrices to shaders

docs.title: Shaders
docs.text: The AssetTool compiles and verifies Shaders and copy them to the ContentBin directory. Interacting with shaders is done via ShaderHelper classes.

docs.title: ShaderHelper class
docs.text:
On sucessfull build, the AssetTool generates a child class of ShaderHelper combining Shader + name of your shader You're able to customize the namespace on the generated class in the asset manifest.

Both the parent, and any child class generated are declared partial so you are able to extend them as you see fit.

The API of this abstract class includes: 

public void Use() // Set this shader as the current OpenGL program

public void AttachUBO(
    uint bufferHandle, 
    string uniformBlockName, 
    uint binding = 0) //Used for Uniform Buffer Objects

public void Dispose()
ShaderUniform Properties are added to the child class for each uniform found on your shader at compile time. With this approach, uniforms optimized out of shaders don't get generated, which causes C# compilation to fail when trying to access these properties preventing runtime uniform not found errors.

docs.title: ShaderUniform <T>
docs.text: This class allows type checking of a shader uniform using a common Set(T value)

docs.title: ShaderTextureUniform
docs.text: This class allows binding a texture and using it in a shader

docs.title: Example ShaderHelper for a BasicModel.vert and BasicModel.frag shader pair.
docs.text:
using System.Numerics;
using Phoenix.Framework.Rendering.Shaders;
using Phoenix.Framework.AssetImport;

namespace Phoenix.Framework.ShaderHelpers
{
    public partial class ShaderBasicModel : ShaderHelper
    {
        public ShaderUniform<Vector3> CameraPosition {get; private set;}
        public ShaderUniform<float> KA {get; private set;}
        public ShaderUniform<float> KD {get; private set;}
        public ShaderUniform<float> KS {get; private set;}
        public ShaderUniform<Vector3> LightColor {get; private set;}
        public ShaderUniform<Vector3> LightPosition {get; private set;}
        public ShaderTextureUniform TexColor {get; private set;}
        public ShaderUniform<Matrix4x4> World {get; private set;}

        public ShaderBasicModel()
        {
            _shader = AssetLoader.LoadShader("Shaders/basicModel/basicModel");

            CameraPosition = new ShaderUniform<Vector3>(_shader, "CameraPosition");
            KA = new ShaderUniform<float>(_shader, "KA");
            KD = new ShaderUniform<float>(_shader, "KD");
            KS = new ShaderUniform<float>(_shader, "KS");
            LightColor = new ShaderUniform<Vector3>(_shader, "LightColor");
            LightPosition = new ShaderUniform<Vector3>(_shader, "LightPosition");
            TexColor = new ShaderTextureUniform(_shader, "TexColor", 0);
            World = new ShaderUniform<Matrix4x4>(_shader, "World");
        }
    }
}

docs.title: Textures
docs.text: Phoenix handles textures through GLTexture objects and are loaded the Asset loading system.

docs.title: UI & ImGui
docs.text: Phoenix integrates ImGui for overlay rendering, plus a custom text-drawing system and centralized error reporting.

docs.title: UI System
docs.text: The UI class is automatically created by PhoenixGame.

docs.title: Drawing Text
docs.text:
// Draw text at position
UI.DrawText("Hello World", new Vector2(100, 100), new Vector4(1, 1, 1, 1), size: 24);

// Centered text
UI.DrawCenteredText("Score: 100", new Vector2(200, 50), new Vector4(1, 1, 1, 1), 32);

// Horizontally centered (right edge at x)
UI.DrawHCenteredText("Centered", new Vector2(400, 50), new Vector4(1, 1, 1, 1), 20);

// Right-aligned (right edge at x)
UI.DrawRAlignedText("FPS: 60", new Vector2(800, 10), new Vector4(1, 1, 1, 1), 16);

docs.title: Drawing Images
docs.text:
// Draw by asset name (resolves from AssetLoader)
UI.DrawImg("textures/ui-panel", new Vector2(0, 0), new Vector2(800, 600));

// With UV cropping
UI.DrawImg("textures/sprite-sheet",
    new Vector2(0, 0),       // screen position
    new Vector2(64, 64),     // screen size
    new Vector2(0, 0),       // UV min
    new Vector2(0.5f, 1f));  // UV max (draws right half only)

// From a GLTexture object
UI.DrawImg(myTexture, new Vector2(100, 100), new Vector2(128, 128));

// From raw OpenGL handle
UI.DrawImg(myTexture.Handle, new Vector2(256, 256),
    new Vector2(50, 50), new Vector2(64, 64),
    new Vector2(200, 200), new Vector2(100, 100));

docs.title: Simple Buttons
docs.text:
UI.DrawSimpleButton("Click Me", new Vector2(100, 100), new Vector2(120, 40), () =>
{
    // Action on click
    Debug.Log("Button clicked!");
});

docs.title: Custom ImGui
docs.text:
Full ImGui access is available through its static class. inside a RenderUI() override:

protected override void RenderUI()
{
    ImGui.Begin("Debug Panel");
    ImGui.Text($"FPS: {Graphics.FPS_SAMPLE:F0}");
    ImGui.Text($"Frame time: {Graphics.FT_SAMPLE * 1000:F1}ms");
    ImGui.Text($"Models loaded: {_models.Count}");
    ImGui.End();
}

docs.title: Font Management
docs.text:
Phoenix loads Cascadia Mono at sizes 10-100 in 1px steps by default. Load additional fonts:

// Load a custom TTF font
UI.LoadFontTTF("fonts/myfont.ttf", new int[] { 16, 24, 32, 48 });

// Push a specific size
UI.SetFontSize(24);
UI.DrawText("Large text", new Vector2(100, 100), Vector4.One, 24);

docs.title: ErrorListWindow
docs.text:
Centralized error reporting from all framework subsystems and you, the user. Errors are displayed as an overlay and logged. Useful for errors that may not be critical, but helpful to be seen at runtime. Goes well together with rendering halt, as UI is always kept active.

// Add with caller info (auto-filled by compiler attributes)
ErrorListWindow.Add("Something went wrong");

// Add with auto-expire (hidden after N seconds)
ErrorListWindow.Add("Temporary warning", showTimeSeconds: 5f);

// Toggle visibility
ErrorListWindow.Show = true;

docs.title: Error Display
docs.text: Each error shows: - Message — the error text - Count — how many times it was added (deduplication) - Hover tooltip — shows [CallerFilePath], [CallerLineNumber], [CallerMemberName]

docs.title: Example Error Output
docs.text:
⚠ Shader error: Could not find uniform "uEmissiveMap" in shader "shaders/pbr"
   at MyGame.Render() in C:\projects\MyGame\MyGame.cs:42

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Shaders — for uniform and texture management
  • Logging — ErrorListWindow and Log system
  • Core Game Loop — when RenderUI() is called

docs.title: Logging
docs.text: Phoenix provides two logging mechanisms: Log (file-based) and ErrorListWindow (in-game overlay).

docs.title: Log (File-Based)
docs.text: Thread-safe file logging to {BaseDirectory}/LOG.txt.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Log.Enabled = true;      // Enable logging
Log.Verbose = true;      // Include verbose messages
Log.ConsoleWrite = true; // Also write to console
Log.Date = true;         // Include date in log entries
Log.Time = true;         // Include time in log entries

docs.title: Log Levels
docs.text:
// Informational
Log.Info("Game started");
Log.Info("Loaded {0} models", modelCount);

// Warnings
Log.Warn("Low disk space, may fail to save");

// Errors
Log.Error("Failed to load texture: textures/missing.png");

// Debug
Log.Debug("Processing frame {0}", frameCount);

// Exceptions
Log.Exception("Crash occurred", ex);

docs.title: Clearing the Log
docs.text: Log.ClearLog();

docs.title: Auto-Enable on Exception
docs.text:
When PhoenixGame.Run() catches an unhandled exception, it automatically enables logging with all flags and writes the exception to the log file.

// LOG.txt (example):
[2026-05-10 14:32:01] ERROR: Failed to load shader: shaders/pbr
   at GLShader.LoadShader(ShaderType type, string path)
   at AssetLoader.LoadShader(string name)

docs.title: Thread Safety
docs.text: Log writes are protected by a lock statement. However, File.AppendAllText opens and closes the file per-write, which may cause issues in multi-process scenarios.

docs.title: ErrorListWindow (In-Game Overlay)
docs.text: Centralized error reporting displayed as an ImGui overlay. Errors from all subsystems are collected here.

docs.title: Visibility
docs.text:
ErrorListWindow.Show = true;   // Show overlay (default: false)
ErrorListWindow.Show = false;  // Hide overlay

docs.title: Adding Errors
docs.text:
Errors are auto-added by the framework. You can also add custom errors:

// Simple error — caller info auto-filled
ErrorListWindow.Add("Something went wrong");

// With auto-expire (hidden after N seconds)
ErrorListWindow.Add("Temporary issue", showTimeSeconds: 5f);

// With explicit caller info
ErrorListWindow.Add(
    "Custom error message",
    "MyGame.cs",       // filePath
    42,                 // lineNumber
    "MyMethod",         // memberName
    0                   // showTimeSeconds (0 = persistent)
);

docs.title: How Errors Are Deduplicated
docs.text:
Adding the same error message twice increments the count instead of creating a new entry:

ErrorListWindow.Add("Shader compile failed");
ErrorListWindow.Add("Shader compile failed");
// Shows: "Shader compile failed" ×2

docs.title: Error Display
docs.text: Each error entry shows: - Error icon and message - Count (if deduplicated) - Hover tooltip showing caller file, line number, and method name

docs.title: Which Systems Report Errors
docs.text: System When It Reports GLShader Uniform not found (unless ignoreUniformsNotFound) GLTexture Texture loading failures SoundManager OpenAL device/context errors Graphics State management errors AssetLoader Asset not found in manifest GLCompiler Shader compile/link failures

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
public class MyGame : PhoenixGame
{
    protected override void Initialize()
    {
        // Enable file logging
        Log.Enabled = true;
        Log.Verbose = true;
        Log.Date = true;
        Log.Time = true;

        // Show error overlay in debug builds
#if DEBUG
        ErrorListWindow.Show = true;
#endif

        Log.Info("Game initialized");
    }

    protected override void Update(double dt)
    {
        try
        {
            // Game logic
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Log.Exception("Update loop error", ex);
            ErrorListWindow.Add($"Update error: {ex.Message}");
        }
    }

    protected override void Render(double dt)
    {
        try
        {
            // Rendering
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Log.Exception("Render loop error", ex);
            ErrorListWindow.Add($"Render error: {ex.Message}");
        }
    }

    protected override void OnClose()
    {
        Log.Info("Game closing");
        Log.ClearLog();
        base.OnClose();
    }
}

docs.title: Log File Format
docs.text:
Each log line format:

[{Date} {Time}] {Level}: {Message}

Example:

[2026-05-10 14:32:01] INFO: Game started
[2026-05-10 14:32:02] WARN: Low disk space
[2026-05-10 14:32:05] ERROR: Shader compile failed
[2026-05-10 14:32:05] EXCEPTION: System.Exception: Shader compile failed
